title: salix sitchensis f. coulteri
intro: Salix sitchensis f. coulteri, also known as Coulter's willow, is a subspecies of the Sitka willow tree, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its salicylic acid content, similar to that found in aspirin. The health benefits of Coulter's willow include relief from pain, fever reduction, and treatment of various infections, such as sore throats and skin conditions. Coulter's willow can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
